summary: The board authorized Goldman Sachs to communicate to Hudson's Bay that the board would not agree to an acquisition at a price below $16 per share and that significant open issues in the draft merger agreement needed to be resolved.
type_fields: {"attachments": [], "binding_level": null, "due_date": null, "other_detail": "Board communicated $16 per share floor and merger agreement resolution requirements", "recipient_refs": ["bidder_hudsons_bay"], "requested_submission": "other"}

summary: In the evening of July 23, there was no indication from Sponsor A or Sponsor E that they were prepared to increase their indicated price beyond their initially indicated range of $14.50 to $15.50 per share, nor did it appear likely they would conclude their diligence more quickly than previously indicated.
type_fields: {"other_detail": null, "related_observation_id": "obs_evt_018", "status_kind": "cannot_improve"}

summary: Hudson's Bay advised Goldman Sachs that, subject to negotiating a definitive binding agreement, it was prepared to offer $16 per share and was agreeable to Saks' position on substantially all significant issues in the draft merger agreement and financing documents.
type_fields: {"delivery_mode": "oral", "includes_draft_merger_agreement": false, "includes_markup": false, "mentions_non_binding": false, "other_detail": "Subject to negotiating definitive binding agreement", "requested_by_observation_id": "obs_evt_023", "revises_observation_id": "obs_evt_017", "terms": {"consideration_type": "cash", "enterprise_value": null, "per_share": "16.0", "range_high": null, "range_low": null}}

summary: The board held a special telephonic meeting attended by Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and Wachtell Lipton to review the terms of the Hudson's Bay proposal.

summary: The go-shop period ended on September 6, 2013. No party was designated as an excluded party. Of 58 parties contacted, six expressed interest and only Company I executed a confidentiality agreement. No acquisition proposals were submitted.
